query: Lee Jae-myung approval rating Gallup Korea Realmeter late June early July 2026, is approval still falling, what is driving the decline, any dead cross
answer:
Research complete. Here's the bottom line on Lee Jae-myung's approval trajectory in late June/early July 2026:

**Is approval still falling?** No — the multi-week slide **halted and modestly reversed in the first week of July** across both major trackers, though from a much lower base than the spring peaks.

**Dead cross?** **Yes, in the ARS trackers.** Realmeter recorded the first post-inauguration dead cross on June 22 (approval 46.7% vs disapproval 49.7%) and it persisted into July (47.0% vs 49.2%, a 3rd consecutive week). KSOI also showed one (47.7% vs 48.2%). **Gallup Korea did NOT** — its methodology kept approval well above disapproval (54% vs 36%).

**The Gallup vs. Realmeter gap** (54% vs 47%) is the key nuance: live-interviewer polling (Gallup) shows no dead cross, while wireless-ARS polls (Realmeter, KSOI) do.

**What drove the decline:**
- The **June 3 local elections** were the inflection point — election-management failures and National Election Commission controversy
- **Ruling Democratic Party factional infighting** (party convention/leadership) depressing the base's enthusiasm
- **Economic anxiety**: high exchange rate/won weakness, stock market plunge, and earlier "excessive welfare/spending" concerns

The early-July rebound was linked to the government's "three mega projects" (semiconductor/AI/data center) regional-investment announcement.

Full structured findings with all data points and sources have been recorded.
